title: Epidemic Spread Sandbox
description: A 2D particle-based SIR epidemic simulation featuring autonomous agent collision mechanics, probabilistic proximity transmission, social distancing velocity damping, real-time stacked line graph rendering, and interactive parameter tuning — all in native JavaScript with Canvas API.
author: Girish Madarkar
features: Autonomous particle system: N agents each with (x,y,vx,vy,state,timer,frozen), bounced off bounding rect with edge reflection, elastic circle-circle collision with velocity swap and overlap separation
SIR state machine: Susceptible (#00ff66) → Infected (#ff2a5f) → Recovered (#8a2be2). Single infected seed at init, timer-based recovery transition when timer ≥ recovery slider threshold
Probabilistic transmission: on S+I overlap, Math.random() < transPct/100 gates infection toggle, configurable 5–100% transmission rate via slider
Social distancing damping: quarSlider freezes quarPct × N randomly selected particles (vx=vy=0, alpha=0.4). Frozen particles reflect colliders. Live flattening feedback through graph curves

Real-time stacked area graph: every 8th frame records S/I/R counts, rendered on secondary canvas as filled+stroked curves with color-matched neon layers (green/red/purple), scrolling 300-sample window
60fps rAF loop: updateParticles() drives physics tick, drawParticles() renders with infected glow (shadowBlur=8 #ff2a5f), telemetry updates at snapshot interval
Two-button control: 'Initialize Simulation' seeds population with parameters; 'Clear Field' empties particle array and resets graph history without page refresh
